description: A Reddit signal from r/Parenting about a mom asking 'how do I tell my 4yo about her absent dad?' was incorrectly routed to ai_magic by the Strategist. The Strategist's notes_for_content_gen contained 'Show the prompt e.g., "how do I tell..."', which the Content Agent then treated as a real AI artifact. The resulting content_queue row had a fabricated original_prompt and original_output that did not exist anywhere in the source.

research_briefing_opp.topic: Telling young kids about absent parent
research_briefing_opp.angle: Mom asks the group how to explain dad's absence to her 4yo. Painful, raw — perfect parenting_insights trust content.
research_briefing_opp.reasoning: High emotional resonance, broad audience, no preachy reframe needed.
research_briefing_opp.suggested_hook: When your kid asks where their dad is and you have no good answer.

strategist_invented_briefing_opp.topic: AI helps mom explain absent dad
strategist_invented_briefing_opp.angle: Show ChatGPT writing a script for telling a 4yo about her absent dad.
strategist_invented_briefing_opp.suggested_hook: I asked AI the impossible mom question and it actually helped.

strategist_notes_fabrication: For row 6d65fbae, show the prompt e.g., 'how do I tell my 4yo about her absent dad?' as the AI input. Sample output: a gentle, age-appropriate script the mom can read. This will land as wow content.

contentgen_fabricated_post.ai_magic_output:
PROMPT: how do I tell my 4yo about her absent dad?
OUTPUT: Hi sweetie, Dad isn't here right now. He loves you very much...
contentgen_fabricated_post.hook: I asked AI the impossible mom question
contentgen_fabricated_post.caption: When you don't have the words, AI does.
